mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for developers and edge computing. It is easy to install and manage, making it ideal for local development and CI/CD environments. However, it is limited to single-node setups and may not be suitable for production-grade multi-node clusters.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using the Snap package manager, which is the recommended method for installation.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Use --classic for full access to system resources.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without needing sudo.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

```

> Replace $USER with your username if needed.

steps.title: Verify MicroK8s Installation
steps.description:
Check the status of MicroK8s to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

microk8s status --wait-ready

```

> This command waits until MicroK8s is fully initialized.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
Enable essential add-ons like DNS and the Kubernetes dashboard for better functionality.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables the DNS add-on.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the Kubernetes nodes are up and running.

```bash

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources managed by MicroK8s.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This will remove MicroK8s and all associated data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations. This step will upgrade MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s status

```

> This command upgrades MicroK8s to the latest stable version.
> Verify that MicroK8s is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s may not have started correctly after installation.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo microk8s start' to start the MicroK8s service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command is not linked to your PATH.

**Fix:**
Use 'sudo microk8s kubectl' or add the microk8s user group to your user.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: localhost:8080'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server is not running.

**Fix:**
Check the status with 'microk8s status' and start it if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to enable addon'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The required permissions or dependencies for the add-on are missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have the necessary permissions and try enabling the add-on again.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features, improved add-on management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with their statuses when running 'microk8s kubectl get nodes'. The output should show the node name and its status as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for running MicroK8s.
